info.title: PostHog React Native SDK
info.description: PostHog React Native SDK allows you to capture events and send them to PostHog from your React Native applications.

functions.description:
PostHogProvider is a React component that provides PostHog functionality to your React Native app. You can find all configuration options in the [React Native SDK docs](https://posthog.com/docs/libraries/react-native#configuration-options).
Autocapturing navigation requires further configuration. See the [React Native SDK navigation docs](https://posthog.com/docs/libraries/react-native#capturing-screen-views) for more information about autocapturing navigation.
This is the recommended way to set up PostHog for React Native. This utilizes the Context API to pass the PostHog client around, enable autocapture.
functions.title: PostHogProvider

examples.name: Add to App.(js|ts)
Using with existing client

params.name: { children, client, options, apiKey, autocapture, style, debug, }

functions.description:
Creates a new PostHog instance for React Native. You can find all configuration options in the [React Native SDK docs](https://posthog.com/docs/libraries/react-native#configuration-options).
If you prefer not to use the PostHogProvider, you can initialize PostHog in its own file and import the instance from there.
functions.title: PostHog

examples.name: posthog.ts

params.description: Your PostHog API key
params.name: apiKey

params.description: PostHog configuration options
params.name: options

functions.description:
Records a breadcrumb-style exception step. Steps accumulate in a rolling, byte-bounded buffer and are attached to every captured `$exception` as `$exception_steps`, giving the error tracking UI a timeline of recent activity before each error.
The `$timestamp` is captured at call time. The reserved keys `$message` and `$timestamp` are stripped from `properties` — the SDK sets the canonical values. This method never throws.
functions.title: addExceptionStep

params.description: A non-empty description of the step
params.name: message

params.description: Optional additional context to attach to the step
params.name: properties

functions.description:
Assigns an alias to the current user.
Sometimes, you want to assign multiple distinct IDs to a single user. This is helpful when your primary distinct ID is inaccessible. For example, if a distinct ID used on the frontend is not available in your backend.
functions.title: alias

examples.name: set alias for current user
examples.code:
// set alias for current user
posthog.alias('distinct_id')

params.description: The alias to assign to the current user
params.name: alias

functions.description: Capture a caught exception manually
functions.title: captureException

examples.name: Capture a caught exception
With additional properties

params.description: The error to capture
params.name: error

params.description: Any additional properties to add to the error event
params.name: additionalProperties

functions.description:
Captures a structured log record and sends it to PostHog's logs product (`/i/v1/logs`). Low-level primitive — most callers will prefer `posthog.logger.info(...)` / `.warn(...)` / `.error(...)` etc., which wrap this with a level pre-set.
Records are buffered per-session, rate-limited, batched into OTLP payloads, and flushed on a timer, on AppState change, or when the buffer reaches capacity. Configure flush cadence, rate cap, and a `beforeSend` filter via the `logs` option on `new PostHog(...)`.
Note — naming collision: `posthog.captureLog()` (this method) is the **logs product** API. There is also a separate, pre-existing `sessionReplayConfig.captureLog` boolean that controls whether **session replay** records the device's `console.*` output. The two are unrelated: this method emits structured records to the logs pipeline regardless of whether session replay is on.
functions.title: captureLog

params.description: Log record. `body` is required; `level` defaults to `'info'`. `attributes` are attached as OTLP key-value attributes and will override auto-populated ones (distinctId, sessionId) on key conflict.
params.name: options

functions.description:
Creates a person profile for the current user, if they don't already have one.
This is useful when using `personProfiles: 'identified_only'` mode and you want to explicitly create a profile for an anonymous user before they identify.
If `personProfiles` is 'identified_only' and no profile exists, this will create one. If `personProfiles` is 'never', this will log an error and do nothing. If `personProfiles` is 'always' or a profile already exists, this is a no-op.
functions.title: createPersonProfile

examples.name: Create a person profile for an anonymous user
examples.code:
// Create a person profile for an anonymous user
posthog.createPersonProfile()

functions.description:
Manually flushes the event queue.
You can set the number of events in the configuration that should queue before flushing. Setting this to 1 will send events immediately and will use more battery. This is set to 20 by default. You can also manually flush the queue. If a flush is already in progress it returns a promise for the existing flush.
Note: this drains the **events** pipeline only. Logs are flushed via , and  drains both before terminating.
functions.title: flush

examples.name: manually flush the queue
examples.code:
// manually flush the queue
await posthog.flush()

functions.description:
Manually flushes the logs queue.
Logs flush automatically on a timer, when the buffer fills, or on AppState change — most apps never need to call this. Use it when you want a synchronous-style hand-off (e.g. before navigating away from a critical screen, in a custom crash handler, or while testing locally).
If a flush is already in progress, both callers join the same in-flight promise — no double-send.
Note: this drains the **logs** pipeline only. Events are flushed via , and  drains both before terminating.
functions.title: flushLogs

functions.description: Returns the stable device identifier used for device-level feature flag bucketing. This ID persists across identify() and reset() calls, only changing on a fresh app install, manual cache clearing, or OS-initiated storage cleanup.
functions.title: getDeviceId

functions.description:
Gets the current user's distinct ID.
You may find it helpful to get the current user's distinct ID. For example, to check whether you've already called identify for a user or not. This returns either the ID automatically generated by PostHog or the ID that has been passed by a call to identify().
functions.title: getDistinctId

examples.name: get current distinct ID
examples.code:
// get current distinct ID
const distinctId = posthog.getDistinctId()

functions.description:
Gets the value of a feature flag for the current user.
Defaults to undefined if not loaded yet or if there was a problem loading. Multivariant feature flags are returned as a string.
functions.title: getFeatureFlag

examples.name: get feature flag value
examples.code:
// get feature flag value
const value = posthog.getFeatureFlag('key-for-your-boolean-flag')

params.description: The feature flag key
params.name: key

params.description: Optional per-call settings
params.name: options

functions.description:
Gets the payload of a feature flag for the current user.
Returns JsonType or undefined if not loaded yet or if there was a problem loading.
functions.title: getFeatureFlagPayload

examples.name: get feature flag payload
examples.code:
// get feature flag payload
const payload = posthog.getFeatureFlagResult('key-for-your-multivariate-flag')?.payload

params.description: The feature flag key
params.name: key

functions.description:
Associates the current user with a group.
Group analytics allows you to associate the events for that person's session with a group (e.g. teams, organizations, etc.). This is a paid feature and is not available on the open-source or free cloud plan.
functions.title: group

examples.name: associate with a group and update properties

params.description: The type of group (e.g. 'company', 'team')
params.name: groupType

params.description: The unique identifier for the group
params.name: groupKey

params.description: Optional properties to set for the group
params.name: properties

functions.description: Associates events with a specific user. Learn more about [identifying users](https://posthog.com/docs/product-analytics/identify)
functions.title: identify

examples.name: Basic identify
Using $set and $set_once

params.description: A unique identifier for your user. Typically either their email or database ID.
params.name: distinctId

params.description: Optional dictionary with key:value pairs to set the person properties
params.name: properties

params.description: Optional capture options
params.name: options

functions.description:
Checks if a feature flag is enabled for the current user.
Defaults to undefined if not loaded yet or if there was a problem loading, unless `defaultValue` is provided.
functions.title: isFeatureEnabled

examples.name: check if feature flag is enabled
examples.code:
// check if feature flag is enabled
const isEnabled = posthog.isFeatureEnabled('key-for-your-boolean-flag')

examples.name: fall back to false while flags are not loaded
examples.code:
// fall back to false while flags are not loaded
const isEnabled = posthog.isFeatureEnabled('key-for-your-boolean-flag', { defaultValue: false })

params.description: The feature flag key
params.name: key

params.description: Optional per-call settings
params.name: options

functions.description:
Returns whether session replay is currently active.
Note: This is only available on iOS and Android. On web/macOS, this always returns false.
functions.title: isSessionReplayActive

functions.description:
Opts the user in to data capture.
By default, PostHog has tracking enabled unless it is forcefully disabled by default using the option  defaultOptIn: false . Once this has been called it is persisted and will be respected until optOut is called again or the reset function is called.
functions.title: optIn

functions.description:
Opts the user out of data capture.
You can completely opt-out users from data capture. Once this has been called it is persisted and will be respected until optIn is called again or the reset function is called.
functions.title: optOut

functions.description:
Registers super properties that are sent with every event.
Super properties are properties associated with events that are set once and then sent with every capture call. They persist across sessions and are stored locally.
functions.title: register

examples.name: register super properties

params.description: An associative array of properties to store about the user
params.name: properties

functions.description:
Reloads feature flags from the server.
PostHog loads feature flags when instantiated and refreshes whenever methods are called that affect the flag. If you want to manually trigger a refresh, you can call this method.
functions.details: When the `disableRemoteFeatureFlags` option is set, this method is a no-op; supply flag values via `updateFlags()` instead.
functions.title: reloadFeatureFlags

functions.description:
Reloads feature flags from the server asynchronously.
PostHog loads feature flags when instantiated and refreshes whenever methods are called that affect the flag. If you want to manually trigger a refresh and get the result, you can call this method.
functions.details: When the `disableRemoteFeatureFlags` option is set, no request is made and the promise resolves with the currently stored flags (from `bootstrap` or `updateFlags()`).
functions.title: reloadFeatureFlagsAsync

examples.name: reload feature flags and get result
examples.code:
// reload feature flags and get result
posthog.reloadFeatureFlagsAsync().then((refreshedFlags) => console.log(refreshedFlags))

functions.description:
Resets the user's ID and anonymous ID after logout.
To reset the user's ID and anonymous ID, call reset. Usually you would do this right after the user logs out. This also clears all stored super properties and more.
By default (when `propertiesToKeep` is not provided), the app lifecycle properties (`InstalledAppBuild` and `InstalledAppVersion`) are automatically preserved to prevent duplicate "Application Installed" events on the next app launch.
If you pass `propertiesToKeep` explicitly, only the properties you specify will be preserved. To keep the default app lifecycle behavior, include `PostHogPersistedProperty.InstalledAppBuild` and `PostHogPersistedProperty.InstalledAppVersion` in your array.
Note: The event queue (`PostHogPersistedProperty.Queue`) and logs queue (`PostHogPersistedProperty.LogsQueue`) are always preserved regardless of what is passed in `propertiesToKeep`, to ensure in-flight data is not lost.
The project-level remote config (`PostHogPersistedProperty.RemoteConfig`, `PostHogPersistedProperty.SessionReplay`, `PostHogPersistedProperty.Surveys`) is also always preserved — it is not user data — so session replay and surveys keep working after an identity change. The user-specific survey state (`SurveysSeen`, `SurveyLastSeenDate`) is still cleared.
functions.title: reset

examples.name: reset after logout (preserves app lifecycle properties by default)
examples.code:
// reset after logout (preserves app lifecycle properties by default)
posthog.reset()

examples.name: reset but keep feature flag overrides and app lifecycle properties

params.description: Optional array of persisted properties to preserve during reset. When not provided, app lifecycle and device bucketing properties are automatically preserved. When provided, only the specified properties are preserved. The event queue and logs queue are always preserved regardless.
params.name: propertiesToKeep

functions.description: Resets group properties for feature flag evaluation.
functions.title: resetGroupPropertiesForFlags

examples.name: reset group properties for flags
examples.code:
// reset group properties for flags
posthog.resetGroupPropertiesForFlags()

params.description: Whether to reload feature flags after setting the properties. Defaults to true.
params.name: reloadFeatureFlags

functions.description: Resets person properties for feature flag evaluation.
functions.title: resetPersonPropertiesForFlags

examples.name: reset person properties for flags
examples.code:
// reset person properties for flags
posthog.resetPersonPropertiesForFlags()

params.description: Whether to reload feature flags after setting the properties. Defaults to true.
params.name: reloadFeatureFlags

functions.description: Captures a screen view event.
functions.details: This function requires a name. You may also pass in an optional properties object. Screen name is automatically registered for the session and will be included in subsequent events.
functions.title: screen

examples.name: Screen capture with properties

params.description: The name of the screen
params.name: name

params.description: Optional properties to include with the screen event
params.name: properties

params.description: Optional capture options
params.name: options

functions.description:
Sets group properties for feature flag evaluation.
These properties are automatically attached to the current group (set via posthog.group()). When you change the group, these properties are reset.
functions.title: setGroupPropertiesForFlags

examples.name: set group properties for flags
examples.code:
// set group properties for flags
posthog.setGroupPropertiesForFlags({'company': {'property1': 'value', property2: 'value2'}})

params.description: The group properties to set for flag evaluation
params.name: properties

params.description: Whether to reload feature flags after setting the properties. Defaults to true.
params.name: reloadFeatureFlags

functions.description: Sets properties on the person profile associated with the current `distinct_id`. Learn more about [identifying users](https://posthog.com/docs/product-analytics/identify)
functions.details: Updates user properties that are stored with the person profile in PostHog. If `personProfiles` is set to `identified_only` and no profile exists, this will create one.
functions.title: setPersonProperties

examples.name: set user properties
set properties with $set_once
set properties without reloading feature flags
examples.code:
// set properties without reloading feature flags
posthog.setPersonProperties({ plan: 'premium' }, undefined, false)

params.description: Optional: An object of properties to store about the user. These properties will overwrite any existing values for the same keys.
params.name: userPropertiesToSet

params.description: Optional: An object of properties to store about the user. If a property is previously set, this does not override that value.
params.name: userPropertiesToSetOnce

params.description: Whether to reload feature flags after setting the properties. Defaults to true.
params.name: reloadFeatureFlags

functions.description:
Sets person properties for feature flag evaluation.
Sometimes, you might want to evaluate feature flags using properties that haven't been ingested yet, or were set incorrectly earlier. You can do so by setting properties the flag depends on with this call. These are set for the entire session. Successive calls are additive: all properties you set are combined together and sent for flag evaluation.
functions.title: setPersonPropertiesForFlags

examples.name: set person properties for flags
examples.code:
// set person properties for flags
posthog.setPersonPropertiesForFlags({'property1': 'value', property2: 'value2'})

params.description: The person properties to set for flag evaluation
params.name: properties

params.description: Whether to reload feature flags after setting the properties. Defaults to true.
params.name: reloadFeatureFlags

functions.description:
Starts session recording. This method will have no effect if PostHog is not enabled, or if session replay is disabled in your project settings.
Note: This is only available on iOS and Android. On web/macOS, this is a no-op.
Requires `posthog-react-native-session-replay` version 1.3.0 or higher.
functions.title: startSessionRecording

examples.name: Resume the current session recording
examples.code:
// Resume the current session recording
await posthog.startSessionRecording()

examples.name: Start a new session recording
examples.code:
// Start a new session recording
await posthog.startSessionRecording(false)

params.description: Whether to resume recording of current session (true) or start a new session (false). Defaults to true.
params.name: resumeCurrent

functions.description:
Stops the current session recording if one is in progress.
Note: This is only available on iOS and Android. On web/macOS, this is a no-op.
Requires `posthog-react-native-session-replay` version 1.3.0 or higher.
functions.title: stopSessionRecording

functions.description:
Removes a super property so it won't be sent with future events.
Super Properties are persisted across sessions so you have to explicitly remove them if they are no longer relevant.
functions.title: unregister

examples.name: remove a super property
examples.code:
// remove a super property
posthog.unregister('icecream pref')

params.description: The name of the super property to remove
params.name: property

functions.description: Removes properties from the person profile associated with the current `distinct_id`. Learn more about [identifying users](https://posthog.com/docs/product-analytics/identify)
functions.title: unsetPersonProperties

params.description: The name (or names) of the person properties to remove.
params.name: propertyNames

params.description: Whether to reload feature flags after removing the properties. Defaults to true.
params.name: reloadFeatureFlags

functions.description:
Replaces (or merges into) the stored feature flags and payloads with locally supplied values, exactly as if they had been returned by the flags endpoint: the values are persisted, `getFeatureFlag()`/`getFeatureFlagPayload()` read them back, and `onFeatureFlags` listeners fire. Makes no network request.
Intended for apps that evaluate flags outside the SDK (e.g. server-side local evaluation) and push the results in at runtime, typically together with the `disableRemoteFeatureFlags` option so the SDK never fetches flags itself.
functions.details: The values are cleared by `reset()`, so push them again after an identity change.
functions.title: updateFlags

examples.name: replace all stored flags with locally evaluated ones
examples.code:
// replace all stored flags with locally evaluated ones
posthog.updateFlags({ 'my-flag': true, 'multivariate-flag': 'variant-1' })

examples.name: merge into the stored flags instead of replacing them
examples.code:
// merge into the stored flags instead of replacing them
posthog.updateFlags({ 'my-flag': false }, undefined, { merge: true })

params.description: Flag keys mapped to their values (boolean, or a variant string)
params.name: flags

params.description: Optional flag keys mapped to their JSON payloads
params.name: payloads

params.description: Set `merge: true` to merge with the currently stored flags instead of replacing them
params.name: options

functions.description: Capture written user feedback for a LLM trace. Numeric values are converted to strings.
functions.title: captureTraceFeedback

params.description: The trace ID to capture feedback for.
params.name: traceId

params.description: The feedback to capture.
params.name: userFeedback

functions.description: Capture a metric for a LLM trace. Numeric values are converted to strings.
functions.title: captureTraceMetric

params.description: The trace ID to capture the metric for.
params.name: traceId

params.description: The name of the metric to capture.
params.name: metricName

params.description: The value of the metric to capture.
params.name: metricValue

functions.description: Returns all currently cached feature flags as `FeatureFlagResult`s. This is a synchronous read of the flags from the last load (no network request) and does not send a `$feature_flag_called` event.
functions.title: getAllFeatureFlags

functions.description:
Returns the current anonymous ID.
This is the ID assigned to users before they are identified. It's used to track anonymous users and link them to identified users when they sign up.
functions.title: getAnonymousId

examples.name: get the anonymous ID

functions.description: * ** GROUPS *
functions.title: groups

functions.description: Enables or disables debug mode for detailed logging.
functions.details: Debug mode logs all PostHog calls to the console for troubleshooting. This is useful during development to understand what data is being sent.
functions.title: debug

functions.description: * ** SURVEYS *
functions.title: getSurveysStateless

functions.description:
Shuts down the PostHog instance and ensures all events are sent.
Call shutdown() once before the process exits to ensure that all events have been sent and all promises have resolved. Do not use this function if you intend to keep using this PostHog instance after calling it. Use flush() for per-request cleanup instead.
functions.title: shutdown

examples.name: shutdown before process exit

params.description: Maximum time to wait for shutdown in milliseconds
params.name: shutdownTimeoutMs

properties.name: nativeCrashes

FeatureFlagMetadata

Capture the screen name (or route name) and properties

Only used for expo-router,

Used for mutating the screen name and properties
Only used if captureScreens is true

If you create a navigation ref with createNavigationContainerRef, you need to pass the navigation ref
Only used for expo-router and
Deprecated: this is deprecated since it didn't work as expected, check out the `captureScreens` comments for capturing screen views semi-automatically
properties.name: navigationRef

properties.description: If provided overrides the auto-generated event UUID. Must be a valid UUID.
properties.name: uuid

properties.description: If provided overrides the auto-generated timestamp
properties.name: timestamp

properties.description: PostHog API host, usually 'https://us.i.posthog.com' or 'https://eu.i.posthog.com'
properties.name: host

properties.description: The number of events to queue before sending to PostHog (flushing)
properties.name: flushAt

properties.description: The interval in milliseconds between periodic flushes

properties.description: The maximum number of queued messages to be flushed as part of a single batch (must be higher than `flushAt`)
properties.name: maxBatchSize

properties.name: maxQueueSize

properties.description: If set to true the SDK is essentially disabled (useful for local environments where you don't want to track anything)
properties.name: disabled

properties.description: If set to false the SDK will not track until the `optIn` function is called.
properties.name: defaultOptIn

properties.description:
Whether to strip URL fragments (`#...`) from automatically captured URL fields.
Disabled by default for backwards compatibility. Set to `true` to strip hashes from:

- `$current_url` on automatically captured browser events, including `$pageview`
- `$initial_current_url`
- `$session_entry_url`
- `$elements[*].attr__href` and `$external_click_url` for autocapture and dead-click autocapture
- Next.js Pages Router `$pageview` `$current_url`
- web vitals `$current_url`
- logs `url.full`
- conversations `current_url` and `request_url`
- session replay rrweb meta/custom-event `href` URLs
- heatmap data URLs

If your SPA relies on hash-based routes for analytics, enabling this is a breaking behavior change.
If you want to capture hashes selectively, leave this as `false` and use `before_send` to remove
sensitive hash values before events are sent.
properties.name: disable_capture_url_hashes

properties.description: Whether to track that `getFeatureFlag` was called (used by Experiments)
properties.name: sendFeatureFlagEvent

properties.description: Whether to load feature flags when initialized or not
properties.name: preloadFeatureFlags

properties.description:
Advanced: whether to disable fetching and evaluating feature flags from PostHog entirely.

When set to true, `reloadFeatureFlags()` and the reloads triggered by `identify()`,
`group()`, `setPersonPropertiesForFlags()` and `reset()` become no-ops, and any request
to the flags endpoint that still goes out (e.g. to fetch remote config or surveys)
carries `disable_flags: true` so the server skips flag evaluation. Flag values must be
supplied via the `bootstrap` option or `updateFlags()` instead; `getFeatureFlag()` and
related methods keep working against those values. Until `updateFlags()` runs, reads
return their not-loaded defaults, so use `bootstrap` for any flags needed at startup.
Equivalent to the web SDK's `advanced_disable_feature_flags`.

Note: surveys gated on feature flags will not evaluate unless the survey targeting
flags are also provided via `updateFlags()`. This option cannot be toggled at runtime.
`posthog-node` inherits this option but does not implement it (no-op).
properties.name: disableRemoteFeatureFlags

properties.description:
Whether to load remote config when initialized or not
Deprecated: Remote config is now always loaded and this option is a no-op. It will be removed in a future version.
properties.name: disableRemoteConfig

properties.description:
Whether to load surveys when initialized or not
Requires the `PostHogSurveyProvider` to be used
properties.name: disableSurveys

properties.description: Option to bootstrap the library with given distinctId and feature flags
properties.type: { distinctId?: string; isIdentifiedId?: boolean; featureFlags?: Record<string, FeatureFlagValue>; featureFlagPayloads?: Record<string, JsonType>; }
properties.name: bootstrap

properties.description: How many times we will retry HTTP requests
properties.name: fetchRetryCount

properties.description: The delay between HTTP request retries in milliseconds
properties.name: fetchRetryDelay

properties.description: Timeout in milliseconds for any calls
properties.name: requestTimeout

properties.description: Timeout in milliseconds for feature flag calls
properties.name: featureFlagsRequestTimeoutMs

properties.description:
How many times feature flag requests retry after a transient network error.
Set to 0 to disable feature flag request retries.
properties.name: featureFlagsRequestMaxRetries

properties.description: Timeout in milliseconds for remote config calls
properties.name: remoteConfigRequestTimeoutMs

properties.description: For Session Analysis how long before we expire a session in seconds
properties.name: sessionExpirationTimeSeconds

properties.description: Whether to disable GZIP compression
properties.name: disableCompression

properties.description: Whether to disable GeoIP lookups
properties.name: disableGeoip

properties.description: Special flag to indicate ingested data is for a historical migration
properties.name: historicalMigration

properties.description:
Evaluation contexts for feature flags.
When set, only feature flags that have at least one matching evaluation tag
will be evaluated for this SDK instance. Feature flags with no evaluation tags
will always be evaluated.

Examples: ['production', 'web', 'mobile']
properties.name: evaluationContexts

properties.description:
Evaluation environments for feature flags.
Deprecated: Use evaluationContexts instead. This property will be removed in a future version.
properties.name: evaluationEnvironments

properties.description:
Determines when to create Person Profiles for users.

- 'always': Always create a person profile for every user (anonymous and identified).
- 'identified_only': Only create a person profile when the user is identified via identify(), alias(), or group().
  Events captured before identification will NOT have person profiles and will be anonymous events.
- 'never': Never create person profiles. identify(), alias(), and group() will be no-ops.
properties.name: personProfiles

properties.description:
Allows modification or dropping of events before they're sent to PostHog.
If an array is provided, the functions are run in order.
If a function returns null, the event will be dropped.
properties.name: before_send

properties.description:
A list of hostnames for which to inject PostHog tracing headers
(X-POSTHOG-DISTINCT-ID, X-POSTHOG-SESSION-ID) on outgoing `fetch` requests.

Use this to link requests made from your app to session replays and LLM traces
in PostHog. When set, the global `fetch` is patched on initialization and the
headers are added to requests whose hostname matches one of the entries.

Requires the SDK to wire up `patchFetchForTracingHeaders` against this option
(currently supported in posthog-react-native).
properties.name: addTracingHeaders

properties.description: Build number like "1.2.2" or "122"
properties.name: $app_build

properties.description: Name of the app as displayed below the icon like "PostHog"
properties.name: $app_name

properties.description: Namespace of the app usually like "com.posthog.app"
properties.name: $app_namespace

properties.description: Human friendly app version like what a user would see in the app store like "1.2.2"
properties.name: $app_version

properties.description: Manufacturer like "Apple", "Samsung" or "Android"
properties.name: $device_manufacturer

properties.description: Model identifier like "iPhone13,2" or "SM-S921B"
properties.name: $device_model

properties.description: Readable model name like "iPhone 12" or "Samsung Galaxy S24"
properties.name: $device_name

properties.description: Device type ("Mobile" | "Desktop")
properties.name: $device_type

properties.description: Whether the app is running on an emulator/simulator
properties.name: $is_emulator

properties.description: Locale (language) of the device like "en-US"
properties.name: $locale

properties.description: Operating system name like iOS or Android
properties.name: $os_name

properties.description: Operating system version "14.0"
properties.name: $os_version

properties.description: Timezone of the device like "Europe/Berlin"
properties.name: $timezone

types.name: PostHogEventProperties

properties.description: Supported compression algorithms
properties.name: supportedCompression

properties.description: Whether surveys are enabled
properties.name: surveys

properties.description:
Error tracking remote config.
Either a boolean (false = disabled) or a map with configuration.
When a map, `autocaptureExceptions` (boolean) controls whether automatic exception capture is enabled remotely.
properties.name: errorTracking

properties.description:
Capture performance remote config.
Either a boolean (false = disabled) or a map with configuration.
When a map, `network_timing` (boolean) controls whether network timing capture is enabled remotely.
properties.name: capturePerformance

properties.description:
Logs feature remote config. When a map, `captureConsoleLogs` (boolean)
is the local opt-in flag for `console.*` autocapture (read by the JS
SDK's `PostHogLogs` extension to decide whether to load the autocapture
bundle).
properties.name: logs

properties.description:
Server-controlled gate for minimal `$feature_flag_called` events. `true` only when the
project opted in; omitted otherwise. Absence always means full events.
properties.name: minimalFlagCalledEvents

properties.description: Pre-send filter. See  for shape and examples. Configure as a single function or a chain.
properties.name: beforeSend

properties.description: Deployment environment attached as OTLP `deployment.environment` (e.g. `'production'`, `'staging'`, `'dev'`).
properties.name: environment

properties.description: How often the periodic background flush fires (ms). Records also flush eagerly when the buffer fills, on AppState changes (RN), and on `shutdown()`. Lower values trade battery/bandwidth for fresher data. Default: 10000 (RN) / 3000 (browser).
properties.name: flushIntervalMs

properties.description: Max records per outbound POST. Keeps each request under the server's 2 MB cap. On a 413 response, the SDK halves this value, retries the same records, then ramps back up by 1 per healthy send. A 413 on a single-record batch drops the record (it's larger than the server can accept regardless of batch size). Default: 50 (RN) / 100 (browser).
properties.name: maxBatchRecordsPerPost

properties.description: Number of buffered records that triggers an immediate flush. Records also flush on the periodic interval and on `shutdown()`. The queue can grow past this while an async flush is in flight (e.g. during a synchronous burst); a separate, larger memory backstop evicts the oldest only once the queue exceeds the per-interval rate cap. Default: 100.
properties.name: maxBufferSize

properties.description:
Tumbling-window rate cap. Bounds how many records can be captured within a sliding (technically tumbling) time window. Records exceeding the cap are dropped synchronously at `captureLog()` (they never enter the buffer or consume bandwidth). A single warn line is logged per window when the cap is hit.
Defaults are per-SDK; on RN the default is `{ maxLogs: 500, windowMs: 10000 }` (≈50 logs/sec ceiling, tuned for cellular bandwidth).
properties.name: rateCap

properties.description: Extra OTLP resource attributes attached to every record. Spread first; SDK-controlled keys (`service.name`, `telemetry.sdk.*`, RN's `os.*`) are layered on top so users cannot accidentally clobber them. Use the dedicated `serviceName` / `environment` / `serviceVersion` fields to override those keys.
properties.name: resourceAttributes

properties.description: Service name attached to every record as the OTLP `service.name` resource attribute. Used by the Logs UI for filtering / grouping. Defaults to `'unknown_service'` when unset.
properties.name: serviceName

properties.description: Service version attached as OTLP `service.version`. Useful for correlating regressions to specific app releases.
properties.name: serviceVersion

properties.description: The child components to mask from PostHog capture
properties.name: children

properties.description:
Captures app lifecycle events such as Application Installed, Application Updated, Application Opened, Application Became Active and Application Backgrounded. Application Installed and Application Updated events are not supported with persistence set to 'memory'.
true
properties.name: captureAppLifecycleEvents

properties.description: Allows you to provide your own implementation of the common information about your App or a function to modify the default App properties generated
properties.name: customAppProperties

properties.description: Allows you to provide a custom asynchronous storage such as async-storage, expo-file-system or a synchronous storage such as mmkv. If not provided, PostHog will attempt to use the best available storage via optional peer dependencies (async-storage, expo-file-system). If `persistence` is set to 'memory', this option will be ignored.
properties.name: customStorage

properties.description:
If enabled, the session id ($session_id) will be persisted across app restarts. This is an option for back compatibility, so your current data isn't skewed with the new version of the SDK. If this is false, the session id will be always reset on app restart.
false
properties.name: enablePersistSessionIdAcrossRestart

properties.description:
Enable Recording of Session Replays for Android and iOS Requires Record user sessions to be enabled in the PostHog Project Settings
false
properties.name: enableSessionReplay

properties.description: Error Tracking Configuration
properties.name: errorTracking

properties.description:
Logs feature configuration. Enables structured log capture via `posthog.captureLog(...)` or `posthog.logger.info(...)`. Records ship to PostHog's logs product (`/i/v1/logs`) in OTLP format, batched on a timer, AppState change, buffer fill, or manual `flushLogs()`.
Capture is **unconditional** — calling the API ships records as long as the SDK is initialized and the user hasn't opted out. The only blockers are `optedOut`, missing/empty `body`, and missing API key.
All fields below are optional; per-SDK defaults apply (mobile defaults are tuned for cellular bandwidth and battery, ~50 logs/sec ceiling).
properties.name: logs

properties.description: Overrides the language used when rendering translated survey copy. When unset, the SDK falls back to the persisted person property `language` and then the device locale.
properties.name: overrideDisplayLanguage

properties.description:
Allows you to provide the storage type. 'file' will try to load the best available storage, the provided 'customStorage', 'customAsyncStorage' or in-memory storage.
'file'
properties.name: persistence

properties.description: A list of headers that should be sent with requests to the PostHog API.
properties.name: requestHeaders

properties.description: Configuration for Session Replay
properties.name: sessionReplayConfig

properties.description:
Automatically include common device and app properties in feature flag evaluation.
When enabled, the following properties are sent with every /flags request: - $app_version: App version - $app_build: App build number - $app_namespace: App bundle identifier / namespace - $os_name: Operating system name - $os_version: Operating system version - $device_type: Device type (Mobile, Desktop) - $lib: Name of the SDK library - $lib_version: Version of the SDK library
This ensures feature flags that rely on these properties work correctly without waiting for server-side processing of identify() calls.
true
properties.name: setDefaultPersonProperties

properties.description: Your PostHog API key
properties.name: apiKey

properties.description:
Autocapture configuration - can be a boolean or detailed options.
When not set, the default behavior is: - `captureScreens`: true - `captureTouches`: false - `captureAppLifecycleEvents`: true
Set to `true` to enable all autocapture features (including touches). Set to `false` to disable all autocapture features.
properties.name: autocapture

properties.description: The child components to render within the PostHog context
properties.name: children

properties.description: An existing PostHog client instance
properties.name: client

properties.description:
Enable debug mode for additional logging
false
properties.name: debug

properties.name: options

properties.name: style

Enable masking of all sandboxed system views
These may include UIImagePickerController, PHPickerViewController and CNContactPickerViewController
iOS only
properties.name: maskAllSandboxedViews

properties.name: captureLog

properties.description:
Deboucer delay used to reduce the number of snapshots captured and reduce performance impact
This is used for capturing the view as a screenshot
The lower the number more snapshots will be captured but higher the performance impact
Deprecated: please use throttleDelayMs instead
properties.name: iOSdebouncerDelayMs

properties.description:
Deboucer delay used to reduce the number of snapshots captured and reduce performance impact
This is used for capturing the view as a screenshot
The lower the number more snapshots will be captured but higher the performance impact
Ps: it was 500ms (0.5s) by default until version 3.3.7
Deprecated: please use throttleDelayMs instead
properties.name: androidDebouncerDelayMs

properties.description:
Throttling delay used to reduce the number of snapshots captured and reduce performance impact
This is used for capturing the view as a wireframe or screenshot
The lower the number more snapshots will be captured but higher the performance impact

This value has precedence over iOSdebouncerDelayMs and androidDebouncerDelayMs
If this is not set, we will take the max number between iOSdebouncerDelayMs and androidDebouncerDelayMs for back compatibility
properties.name: throttleDelayMs

properties.description:
Enable capturing network telemetry
iOS only
properties.name: captureNetworkTelemetry

properties.description:
Schedule screenshot image capture on a background queue.
iOS only
Experimental support

Warning: Enabling this option will trigger Main Thread Checker warnings and may
briefly freeze the app the first time a screenshot is captured. Consider disabling
Main Thread Checker in your scheme's run diagnostics when enabling this.
properties.name: screenshotModeBackgroundCapture

properties.description:
Session replay sample rate between 0 and 1
Local config has precedence over remote config when both are set.
If undefined, sampling is controlled by remote config (when available).
properties.name: sampleRate

properties.name: defaultSurveyAppearance

properties.description: If true, PosHog appearance will be ignored and defaultSurveyAppearance is always used.

properties.description:
The keyboard avoiding behavior for the survey modal (KeyboardAvoidingView), applied only to Android devices.
- 'padding': Adds padding to avoid keyboard (recommended)
- 'height': Resizes the view height (default, for legacy - may cause flickering on some Android devices)
